contest.title: Codeforces Round 1092 (Unrated, Div. 1, Based on THUPC 2026 Finals)

sources.notes: Statement and constraints: repeatedly choose intervals of length at least k and apply mod p or mod q.
Luogu mirror used to cross-check statement and constraints.
Used to confirm the first-interval characterization and prefix-sum formula.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate Luogu solution page; not used as evidence in this review.

annotation.summary: Given an array and two mod choices p<q, choose interval operations to minimize the final sum.
annotation.constraints: Sum n <= 1e5 and values/moduli can be large, so an O(n) per total input solution is intended.
annotation.core_idea: Each element can end as either b_i=a_i mod p or c_i=(a_i mod q) mod p. After the first chosen interval, other elements can be independently pushed to min(b_i,c_i). The only coupled decision is the first interval, and lengthening it is never beneficial, so enumerate all length-k first intervals and take min(sum b, sum c) inside plus min(b,c) outside.
annotation.complexity: O(n) per test case with prefix sums.
tricks: first interval is the only global coupling
first interval can be assumed length exactly k
prefix sums evaluate each candidate interval

solution_variants.name: first-interval-enumeration
solution_variants.summary: Precompute b_i, c_i, and prefix sums of b, c, and min(b,c); enumerate every length-k first interval.

tags.evidence: Outside the first interval every element should greedily be reduced to min(b_i,c_i), and the first interval should be exactly length k.
Prefix sums over b, c, and min(b,c) evaluate every candidate interval in O(1).
The reachable values are characterized by compositions of mod p and mod q.
